astronomy.phase.0.brief: The dark seed — invisible, full of potential, everything begins here
astronomy.phase.0.guidance: Set intentions, start projects, rest and reflect in the darkness
astronomy.phase.0.name: New Moon
astronomy.phase.0.theme: Beginnings, seed planting, intention setting, the dark of renewal
astronomy.phase.1.brief: The first sliver — intention made visible, the young light gathering strength
astronomy.phase.1.guidance: Take the first step, gather resources, push through initial resistance
astronomy.phase.1.name: Waxing Crescent
astronomy.phase.1.theme: Emerging intention, first commitment, the courage to begin
astronomy.phase.2.brief: The half-lit choice — light and dark in equal measure, the crossroads
astronomy.phase.2.guidance: Make decisions, overcome obstacles, commit to the direction chosen
astronomy.phase.2.name: First Quarter
astronomy.phase.2.theme: Challenge, decision, the crisis of action
astronomy.phase.3.brief: Almost full — the final preparation, polishing before the peak
astronomy.phase.3.guidance: Refine the plan, adjust based on feedback, trust the process
astronomy.phase.3.name: Waxing Gibbous
astronomy.phase.4.brief: The full light — everything illuminated, nothing hidden, the peak
astronomy.phase.4.guidance: Celebrate completion, see clearly, harvest what was planted at the new moon
astronomy.phase.4.name: Full Moon
astronomy.phase.5.brief: The generous decline — the light begins to give itself away
astronomy.phase.5.guidance: Share what you've learned, give back, express gratitude for the harvest
astronomy.phase.5.name: Waning Gibbous
astronomy.phase.6.brief: The half-lit release — light and dark again equal, but now the light is leaving
astronomy.phase.6.guidance: Let go of what no longer serves, forgive, clear space for the new
astronomy.phase.6.name: Last Quarter
astronomy.phase.6.theme: Release, forgiveness, clearing, the crisis of letting go
astronomy.phase.7.brief: The last whisper — the thinnest light before the dark, the wisdom of surrender
astronomy.phase.7.guidance: Rest deeply, surrender control, prepare for the coming darkness
astronomy.phase.7.name: Waning Crescent
astronomy.phase.7.theme: Surrender, rest, preparation for the void, the balsamic phase

astronomy.planet.1.name: Venus

astronomy.planet.2.name: Earth
astronomy.planet.3.archetype: The Warrior — the red force that fights for what matters

astronomy.planet.4.name: Jupiter
astronomy.planet.5.archetype: The Elder — the ringed boundary, the lesson that cannot be avoided

astronomy.planet.7.archetype: The Mystic — the dissolving ocean where boundaries cease to exist
astronomy.planet.7.brief: The blue dreamer — farthest major planet, where the solar system fades into mystery

astronomy.planet.7.name: Neptune

coptic.feast.1.brief: The Coptic New Year ��� 1 Thout, colored red for the blood of the martyrs
coptic.feast.1.name: Nayrouz
coptic.feast.1.practice: Red vestments, red dates, lighting candles for the martyrs, visiting churches
coptic.feast.1.theme: New Year of the Martyrs ��� remembrance of those who died for faith
coptic.feast.2.brief: The Cross found ��� bonfires mirror the flames Helena lit to find the buried wood
coptic.feast.2.name: Feast of the Cross
coptic.feast.2.practice: Lighting bonfires (demera), singing hymns, outdoor celebration on 17 Thout
coptic.feast.2.theme: Discovery of the True Cross by Empress Helena in Jerusalem
coptic.feast.3.brief: The Word made flesh ��� after 43 days of fasting, the Light enters the world
coptic.feast.3.name: Christmas
coptic.feast.3.practice: Midnight liturgy (29 Koiak / January 7), breaking the fast with festive foods
coptic.feast.3.theme: The Nativity of Christ ��� culmination of the 43-day Advent fast
coptic.feast.4.brief: God revealed in water ��� the dove descends, the voice speaks, the Trinity appears
coptic.feast.4.name: Epiphany
coptic.feast.4.practice: Blessing of waters, baptismal renewal liturgy on 11 Tobi (January 19)
coptic.feast.4.theme: Theophany ��� the Baptism of Christ in the Jordan, God revealed
coptic.feast.5.brief: The angel's greeting ��� the moment when heaven asked earth's permission
coptic.feast.5.name: Annunciation
coptic.feast.5.practice: Joyful liturgy on 29 Paremhat (April 7), special hymns to the Theotokos
coptic.feast.5.theme: The angel Gabriel announces to the Virgin Mary ��� the Incarnation begins
coptic.feast.6.brief: The Resurrection ��� death defeated, the stone rolled away, the first light of Sunday
coptic.feast.6.name: Easter
coptic.feast.6.practice: 55-day Great Lent, midnight Paschal liturgy, breaking fast, red eggs, greeting 'Christ is Risen'
coptic.feast.6.theme: The Resurrection of Christ ��� the feast of feasts, joy of joys
coptic.month.1.brief: Month of Thoth ��� the ancient scribe god opens the new year of the martyrs
coptic.month.1.liturgy: Start of the Coptic year, commemorating the martyrs
coptic.month.1.name: Thout
coptic.month.1.origin: From Egyptian Thoth (Djehuty), god of wisdom and writing
coptic.month.10.brief: Month of Paoni ��� the ancient valley of the dead becomes the apostolic fast
coptic.month.10.liturgy: Apostles' Fast begins, honoring Peter and Paul
coptic.month.10.name: Paoni
coptic.month.10.origin: From Egyptian Pa-en-Inet (valley festival), honoring the dead
coptic.month.11.brief: Month of Epip ��� the fierce heat that tested both ancient Egypt and early Christians
coptic.month.11.liturgy: Summer height, commemoration of saints and martyrs
coptic.month.11.name: Epip
coptic.month.11.origin: From Egyptian Ipip, associated with the great heat
coptic.month.12.brief: Month of Mesori ��� the rebirth of Ra becomes the Assumption of the Theotokos
coptic.month.12.liturgy: Assumption of the Virgin Mary fast and feast
coptic.month.12.name: Mesori
coptic.month.12.origin: From Egyptian Wep-Renpet (birth of Ra), the sun reborn
coptic.month.13.brief: The little month ��� five or six days suspended between years, sacred pause
coptic.month.13.liturgy: Days of prayer and reflection between years, no fasting
coptic.month.13.name: Nasie
coptic.month.13.origin: Epagomenal days (Greek epagomenai), outside the regular months
coptic.month.2.brief: Month of Paopi ��� the sacred festival that honored the river's gift
coptic.month.2.name: Paopi
coptic.month.2.origin: From Egyptian Ipet (Opet festival), sacred barque procession
coptic.month.3.brief: Month of Hathor ��� the cow-goddess of joy gives way to Advent expectation
coptic.month.3.liturgy: Advent preparation begins in the Coptic tradition
coptic.month.3.name: Hathor
coptic.month.3.origin: From Egyptian Hathor, goddess of love, joy, and the sky
coptic.month.4.brief: Month of Koiak ��� the ancient Osiris rites become the fast before Christ's nativity
coptic.month.4.liturgy: Coptic Advent fast, preparation for Christmas (29 Koiak)
coptic.month.4.name: Koiak
coptic.month.4.origin: From Egyptian Ka-her-Ka (soul upon soul), Osiris mysteries
coptic.month.5.brief: Month of Tobi ��� the waters of purification and the baptism of Christ
coptic.month.5.name: Tobi
coptic.month.5.origin: From Egyptian Djebaty, associated with purification
coptic.month.6.brief: Month of Meshir ��� the burning sun that once parched Egypt now warms the faithful
coptic.month.6.liturgy: Ordinary time, parish festivals of local saints
coptic.month.6.name: Meshir
coptic.month.6.origin: From Egyptian Mekhir, month of the burning sun
coptic.month.7.brief: Month of Paremhat ��� the builder-healer's month crowned by the angel's greeting
coptic.month.7.liturgy: The Annunciation (29 Paremhat), feast of the Theotokos
coptic.month.7.name: Paremhat
coptic.month.8.brief: Month of Parmouti ��� the harvest goddess meets the Lenten fast
coptic.month.8.liturgy: Great Lent deepens, approaching Holy Week
coptic.month.8.name: Parmouti
coptic.month.9.brief: Month of Pashons ��� the moon god's month receives the light of resurrection
coptic.month.9.liturgy: Paschal season, Easter and its afterglow
coptic.month.9.name: Pashons
coptic.month.9.origin: From Egyptian Pa-en-Khonsu, moon god of passage

ethiopian.feast.1.name: Enkutatash

ethiopian.feast.2.brief: The Cross revealed ��� 17 Meskerem, when the great bonfire falls toward the buried relic
ethiopian.feast.2.name: Meskel
ethiopian.feast.2.practice: Demera bonfire ceremony, yellow daisies laid in cross pattern, processions with priests and drums
ethiopian.feast.2.theme: Finding of the True Cross ��� light triumphant over darkness
ethiopian.feast.3.brief: Ethiopian Christmas ��� 29 Tahsas, the dawn service after the longest fast
ethiopian.feast.3.name: Genna
ethiopian.feast.3.practice: All-night church service, breaking the fast, genna (field hockey), white shammas, injera feast
ethiopian.feast.3.theme: Christmas ��� the Nativity, preceded by 43 days of fasting
ethiopian.feast.4.brief: Timkat ��� 11 Tir, when the tabots emerge from the churches and the waters are blessed
ethiopian.feast.4.name: Timkat
ethiopian.feast.4.practice: Three-day festival, tabot (Ark replica) processions, blessing of waters, joyful crowds in white
ethiopian.feast.4.theme: Epiphany ��� the Baptism of Christ, the holiest celebration
ethiopian.month.1.brief: First month ��� the rains recede and Ethiopia carpets itself in yellow daisies
ethiopian.month.1.name: Meskerem
ethiopian.month.1.season: End of rainy season (Kiremt), flowers bloom across the highlands
ethiopian.month.10.brief: Tenth month ��� the great rains arrive, rivers swell, the world goes green
ethiopian.month.10.name: Sene

ethiopian.month.11.name: Hamle

ethiopian.month.13.name: Pagume

geology.eon.0.brief: The age of fire â 4.54 to 4.0 billion years ago, Earth forming from the solar nebula
geology.eon.0.character: Molten hell â the bombardment era when Earth was a ball of magma under constant asteroid rain
geology.eon.0.life: No life â surface too hot for water, atmosphere of hydrogen and helium
geology.eon.0.name: Hadean
geology.eon.1.brief: The age of beginnings â 4.0 to 2.5 Ga, when life invented itself in a world without oxygen
geology.eon.1.character: First oceans, first continents â the planet cools enough for liquid water and the first rocks
geology.eon.1.life: First life appears â prokaryotes, stromatolites, the Great Oxidation Event begins
geology.eon.1.name: Archean
geology.eon.2.brief: The age of hidden life â 2.5 Ga to 538 Ma, the longest eon, oxygen slowly transforming everything
geology.eon.2.character: Snowball Earth episodes, oxygen rising â the long preparation for complex life
geology.eon.2.life: Eukaryotes evolve, first multicellular organisms â the Ediacaran fauna at the end
geology.eon.2.name: Proterozoic
geology.eon.3.brief: The age of visible life â 538 Ma to present, everything we recognize as life
geology.eon.3.character: The age we live in â all familiar life, all mass extinctions, all human history
geology.eon.3.life: Cambrian explosion to now â trilobites, dinosaurs, mammals, humans, all in 538 million years
geology.eon.3.name: Phanerozoic
geology.extinction.0.aftermath: Recovery took 5-10 million years; life diversified anew in the Silurian
geology.extinction.0.brief: The ice extinction â 85% of marine species lost when Gondwana froze at the South Pole
geology.extinction.0.cause: Glaciation of Gondwana, sea level drop, ocean anoxia
geology.extinction.0.name: Late Ordovician
geology.extinction.1.aftermath: Armored fish disappeared; tetrapods (our ancestors) survived and walked onto land
geology.extinction.1.brief: The slow extinction â 75% of species lost in pulses over 20 million years
geology.extinction.1.cause: Multiple pulses over 20 My: ocean anoxia, possible volcanism, Appalachian weathering
geology.extinction.1.name: Late Devonian
geology.extinction.2.aftermath: Recovery took 10 million years; the emptied world was inherited by archosaurs leading to dinosaurs
geology.extinction.2.brief: The Great Dying â 96% of all species lost, the worst catastrophe life has ever faced
geology.extinction.2.cause: Siberian Traps volcanism, global warming, ocean acidification and anoxia
geology.extinction.2.name: End-Permian
geology.extinction.3.aftermath: Dinosaurs inherited the emptied niches, beginning their 135-million-year reign
geology.extinction.3.brief: The dinosaur's opportunity â 80% of species lost, clearing the stage for the Jurassic giants
geology.extinction.3.cause: Central Atlantic Magmatic Province volcanism, CO2 spike, ocean acidification
geology.extinction.3.name: End-Triassic
geology.extinction.4.aftermath: Mammals diversified explosively; birds survived as the last dinosaurs; our lineage began
geology.extinction.4.brief: The asteroid â 76% of species including all non-avian dinosaurs, ending one world and beginning ours
geology.extinction.4.cause: Chicxulub asteroid impact + Deccan Traps volcanism, impact winter, global fires
geology.extinction.4.name: End-Cretaceous
geology.supercontinent.0.brief: The first gathering â Earth's earliest attempt to assemble its crust into one mass
geology.supercontinent.0.description: Oldest proposed supercontinent â evidence from South Africa (Kaapvaal) and Australia (Pilbara)
geology.supercontinent.0.name: Vaalbara
geology.supercontinent.1.brief: The continent that breathed â its breakup may have triggered the rise of atmospheric oxygen
geology.supercontinent.1.description: Archean supercontinent that preceded the Great Oxidation Event
geology.supercontinent.1.name: Kenorland
geology.supercontinent.2.brief: The first proven unity â Earth's continents locked together for 500 million years
geology.supercontinent.2.description: Also called Nuna â first well-documented supercontinent, all major cratons joined
geology.supercontinent.2.name: Columbia
geology.supercontinent.3.brief: The continent that froze the world â its rifting led to the most extreme ice ages
geology.supercontinent.3.description: Supercontinent whose breakup may have triggered Snowball Earth glaciations
geology.supercontinent.3.name: Rodinia
geology.supercontinent.4.brief: The fleeting assembly â together and apart in 80 million years, bookending the first complex animals
geology.supercontinent.4.description: Brief assembly at the end of the Ediacaran, just before the Cambrian explosion
geology.supercontinent.4.name: Pannotia
geology.supercontinent.5.brief: The one we remember â the supercontinent that shaped the world we know
geology.supercontinent.5.description: The most famous supercontinent â Permian to Jurassic, all land united
geology.supercontinent.5.name: Pangaea
geology.supercontinent.6.brief: The one to come â the Atlantic will close and the continents will embrace again
geology.supercontinent.6.description: Predicted next supercontinent â Atlantic closing, new assembly in 250 million years
geology.supercontinent.6.name: Pangaea Proxima

iching.hex.18.judgment: What has been corrupted through neglect can be restored through effort
iching.hex.18.name: Work on the Decayed
iching.hex.19.image: Earth above the lake â the sage teaches and shelters without limit
iching.hex.19.judgment: The great approaches with willing condescension toward the small
iching.hex.19.name: Approach
iching.hex.2.image: Earth upon earth â the sage supports all beings with broad virtue
iching.hex.2.judgment: Pure receptive force nourishes all things
iching.hex.2.name: The Receptive
iching.hex.20.image: Wind over earth â the ancient kings visited the regions to teach the people
iching.hex.20.judgment: The sage gazes upon the divine meaning beneath appearances
iching.hex.20.name: Contemplation
iching.hex.21.image: Thunder and lightning â the ancient kings enforced the laws with clarity
iching.hex.21.judgment: Energetic biting through obstacles restores unity
iching.hex.21.name: Biting Through
iching.hex.22.image: Fire at the foot of the mountain â the sage brightens governance without daring to judge
iching.hex.22.judgment: Beauty adorns form but substance must remain within
iching.hex.22.name: Grace
iching.hex.23.image: Mountain resting on the earth â the ruler strengthens the foundation of the people
iching.hex.23.judgment: The dark forces peel away what has been built â accept and wait
iching.hex.23.name: Splitting Apart
iching.hex.24.image: Thunder within the earth â the ancient kings closed the passes at solstice
iching.hex.24.judgment: After decay, the turning point â the one light returns below
iching.hex.24.name: Return
iching.hex.25.image: Thunder under heaven â the ancient kings nourished all beings in accord with the season
iching.hex.25.judgment: Acting from genuine nature without ulterior motive brings success
iching.hex.25.name: Innocence
iching.hex.26.image: Heaven within the mountain â the sage studies the ancients to cultivate character
iching.hex.26.judgment: Firm restraint of great creative power builds reserves of strength
iching.hex.26.name: Great Taming
iching.hex.27.image: Thunder at the foot of the mountain â the sage is careful with words and temperate in eating
iching.hex.27.judgment: Attending to what nourishes body and mind brings good fortune
iching.hex.27.name: Nourishment
iching.hex.28.image: The lake rises above the trees â the sage stands alone without fear
iching.hex.28.judgment: The ridgepole bends â extraordinary measures are needed in extraordinary times
iching.hex.28.name: Great Excess
iching.hex.29.image: Water flows on and reaches the goal â the sage teaches through repeated practice
iching.hex.29.judgment: Repeated danger teaches sincerity of heart â flow like water
iching.hex.29.name: The Abysmal
iching.hex.3.image: Thunder and rain â the sage brings order from chaos
iching.hex.3.judgment: Birth pangs of new endeavors require perseverance
iching.hex.3.name: Difficulty at the Beginning
iching.hex.30.image: Fire doubled produces great brightness â the sage illuminates all regions
iching.hex.30.judgment: Luminous clarity depends on something to cling to â accept dependence
iching.hex.30.name: The Clinging
iching.hex.31.image: The lake upon the mountain â the sage receives others with openness
iching.hex.31.judgment: Mutual attraction between the yielding and the firm brings union
iching.hex.31.name: Influence
iching.hex.32.image: Thunder and wind reinforce each other â the sage stands firm in his direction
iching.hex.32.judgment: Enduring consistency in one's way of life brings success
iching.hex.32.name: Duration
iching.hex.33.image: Mountain under heaven â the sage keeps the inferior at a distance with dignity
iching.hex.33.judgment: Strategic withdrawal before the advancing dark preserves strength
iching.hex.33.name: Retreat
iching.hex.34.image: Thunder above heaven â the sage treads no path contrary to propriety
iching.hex.34.judgment: Great power combined with righteousness moves heaven and earth
iching.hex.34.name: Great Power
iching.hex.35.image: The sun rises over the earth â the sage brightens his bright virtue
iching.hex.35.judgment: The bright sun rises over the earth â willing service brings rapid advance
iching.hex.35.name: Progress
iching.hex.36.image: The sun sinks into the earth â the sage veils his brightness in dealings with the multitude
iching.hex.36.judgment: In times of darkness the sage conceals his light to endure adversity
iching.hex.36.name: Darkening of the Light
iching.hex.37.image: Wind comes from fire â the sage speaks with substance and acts with consistency
iching.hex.37.judgment: The well-ordered family is the foundation of all social order
iching.hex.37.name: The Family
iching.hex.38.image: Fire above the lake â the sage remains individual within community
iching.hex.38.judgment: Despite outward opposition, underlying affinity makes small things possible
iching.hex.38.name: Opposition
iching.hex.39.image: Water on the mountain â the sage turns attention to self-improvement
iching.hex.39.judgment: Facing an abyss before and a mountain behind â turn inward and gather allies
iching.hex.39.name: Obstruction
iching.hex.4.image: Spring at the foot of the mountain â the sage cultivates character
iching.hex.4.judgment: The young fool seeks the teacher, not the teacher the fool
iching.hex.4.name: Youthful Folly
iching.hex.40.image: Thunder and rain arise â the sage pardons errors and forgives transgressions
iching.hex.40.judgment: When release from tension comes, return to normalcy swiftly
iching.hex.40.name: Deliverance
iching.hex.41.image: The lake at the foot of the mountain â the sage controls anger and restrains instincts
iching.hex.41.judgment: Decrease of the lower enriches the higher â sincerity in simplicity brings blessings
iching.hex.41.name: Decrease
iching.hex.42.image: Wind and thunder strengthen each other â the sage moves toward good and corrects faults
iching.hex.42.judgment: The ruler decreases self to increase the people â generous sacrifice brings benefit
iching.hex.42.name: Increase
iching.hex.43.image: The lake rises to heaven â the sage distributes blessings to those below
iching.hex.43.judgment: Resolute action brings dark matters to light â proclaim the danger openly
iching.hex.43.name: Breakthrough
iching.hex.44.image: Wind under heaven â the ruler proclaims his commands to the four quarters
iching.hex.44.judgment: The dark principle advances from below â recognize and contain it early
iching.hex.44.name: Coming to Meet
iching.hex.45.image: The lake over the earth â the sage renews his weapons to meet the unforeseen
iching.hex.45.judgment: People gather around a strong leader â prepare for the unexpected
iching.hex.45.name: Gathering Together
iching.hex.46.image: Within the earth wood grows â the sage builds great things from small beginnings
iching.hex.46.judgment: Gentle effort directed steadily upward meets with supreme success
iching.hex.46.name: Pushing Upward
iching.hex.47.image: The lake has no water â the sage risks his life to fulfill his purpose
iching.hex.47.judgment: Exhaustion of resources tests character â the sage stakes his life on following his will
iching.hex.47.name: Oppression
iching.hex.48.image: Water over wood â the sage encourages the people and counsels mutual aid
iching.hex.48.judgment: The well nourishes all without being exhausted â seek the unchanging source
iching.hex.48.name: The Well
iching.hex.49.image: Fire in the lake â the sage orders the calendar and clarifies the seasons
iching.hex.49.judgment: When the time is ripe, revolutionary change commands belief
iching.hex.49.name: Revolution
iching.hex.5.image: Clouds rise to heaven â the sage eats, drinks, and is joyful
iching.hex.5.judgment: Sincerity and patience lead to crossing the great water
iching.hex.5.name: Waiting
iching.hex.50.image: Fire over wood â the sage consolidates destiny by making his position correct
iching.hex.50.judgment: The sacred vessel transforms raw material into nourishment for the wise
iching.hex.50.name: The Cauldron
iching.hex.51.image: Repeated thunder â the sage sets things in order through fearful reflection
iching.hex.51.judgment: Shock and thunder bring fear, then laughter â the sage examines himself through awe
iching.hex.51.name: The Arousing
iching.hex.52.image: Mountains joined together â the sage does not permit thoughts to go beyond the situation
iching.hex.52.judgment: Stillness of the mountain â the sage quiets the mind and does not go beyond his position
iching.hex.52.name: Keeping Still
iching.hex.53.image: A tree on the mountain grows slowly â the sage abides in dignity and virtue
iching.hex.53.judgment: Gradual development like a tree on the mountain brings lasting results
iching.hex.53.name: Gradual Progress
iching.hex.54.image: Thunder over the lake â the sage understands the transitory in light of eternity
iching.hex.54.judgment: The younger sister follows the elder â knowing one's place brings eventual good
iching.hex.54.name: The Marrying Maiden
iching.hex.55.image: Thunder and lightning together â the sage decides lawsuits and carries out punishments
iching.hex.55.judgment: Fullness and abundance at the zenith â act now for the sun must set
iching.hex.55.name: Abundance
iching.hex.56.image: Fire on the mountain â the sage is clear and cautious in applying penalties
iching.hex.56.judgment: The traveler succeeds through smallness and adaptability, not grandeur
iching.hex.56.name: The Wanderer
iching.hex.57.image: Winds following one another â the sage spreads his commands and carries out his affairs
iching.hex.57.judgment: Persistent gentle penetration achieves what aggression cannot
iching.hex.57.name: The Gentle
iching.hex.58.image: Lakes joined together â the sage joins with friends for discussion and practice
iching.hex.58.judgment: True joy comes from inner firmness expressed with outward gentleness
iching.hex.58.name: The Joyous
iching.hex.59.image: Wind drives over the water â the ancient kings made offerings and built temples
iching.hex.59.judgment: Wind over water disperses rigid separations â use gentle means to dissolve barriers
iching.hex.59.name: Dispersion
iching.hex.6.image: Heaven and water move apart â the sage plans before acting
iching.hex.6.judgment: Inner truth and caution resolve disagreement
iching.hex.6.name: Conflict
iching.hex.60.image: Water over the lake â the sage creates number and measure to examine virtue and conduct
iching.hex.60.judgment: Proper limits bring structure â but bitter limitations should not be held to
iching.hex.60.name: Limitation
iching.hex.61.image: Wind over the lake â the sage deliberates upon punishments and delays execution
iching.hex.61.judgment: Inner truth reaches even pigs and fish â sincerity moves all beings
iching.hex.61.name: Inner Truth
iching.hex.62.image: Thunder on the mountain â the sage is reverent in conduct and sorrowful in mourning
iching.hex.62.judgment: In small matters excess is permissible â the flying bird leaves its song behind
iching.hex.62.name: Small Excess
iching.hex.63.image: Water over fire â the sage reflects on misfortune and prepares against it
iching.hex.63.judgment: All lines in their proper place â success, but vigilance against disorder
iching.hex.63.name: After Completion
iching.hex.64.image: Fire over water â the sage carefully distinguishes things and puts them in their places
iching.hex.64.judgment: The final crossing is not yet accomplished â careful discernment of forces is needed
iching.hex.64.name: Before Completion
iching.hex.7.image: Water within the earth â the sage nurtures the people and gathers strength
iching.hex.7.judgment: Discipline and just leadership organize the people
iching.hex.7.name: The Army
iching.hex.8.image: Water upon the earth â the ancient kings established their states
iching.hex.8.judgment: Union requires a central figure of sincerity
iching.hex.8.name: Holding Together
iching.hex.9.image: Wind over heaven â the sage refines outward form and virtue
iching.hex.9.judgment: Gentle restraint achieves what force cannot
iching.hex.9.name: Small Taming

islamic.month.1.brief: Sacred month of mourning and remembrance
islamic.month.1.name: Muharram
islamic.month.10.brief: Month of celebration and voluntary fasting
islamic.month.10.event: Eid al-Fitr (1st) -- Festival of Breaking Fast
islamic.month.10.name: Shawwal
islamic.month.11.brief: Sacred month of truce and preparation for Hajj
islamic.month.11.name: Dhul Qa'dah
islamic.month.12.brief: Sacred month of pilgrimage and sacrifice
islamic.month.12.event: Hajj (8th-12th), Eid al-Adha (10th) -- Festival of Sacrifice
islamic.month.12.name: Dhul Hijjah
islamic.month.2.brief: Month of travel -- traditionally considered difficult
islamic.month.2.name: Safar
islamic.month.3.brief: Month of joy and prophetic celebration
islamic.month.3.name: Rabi al-Awwal
islamic.month.4.brief: Quiet month of reflection and growth
islamic.month.4.name: Rabi al-Thani
islamic.month.5.name: Jumada al-Ula
islamic.month.6.name: Jumada al-Thani
islamic.month.7.name: Rajab
islamic.month.8.name: Sha'ban
islamic.month.9.brief: Month of fasting, revelation, and spiritual renewal
islamic.month.9.name: Ramadan
islamic.prayer.0.meaning: Awakening -- beginning before the world stirs
islamic.prayer.0.name: Fajr
islamic.prayer.1.meaning: Remembrance -- pausing at the day's peak
islamic.prayer.1.name: Dhuhr
islamic.prayer.2.name: Asr
islamic.prayer.3.name: Maghrib
islamic.prayer.4.name: Isha

kabbalah.path.0.journey: Stepping into the void with perfect trust
kabbalah.path.0.lesson: Faith requires no ground to stand on
kabbalah.path.1.journey: Channeling divine will into conscious creation
kabbalah.path.1.lesson: The creator creates by becoming transparent to creation
kabbalah.path.10.lesson: Fortune changes but passion outlasts every turning
kabbalah.path.11.lesson: Balance is not compromise â it is precision of the heart
kabbalah.path.12.lesson: The deepest discipline is to stop struggling
kabbalah.path.13.lesson: Something must die for desire to become authentic
kabbalah.path.14.lesson: Harmony manifests only when it touches the ground
kabbalah.path.15.lesson: Beauty must include darkness or it becomes a lie
kabbalah.path.16.lesson: When desire and truth collide, every false structure falls
kabbalah.path.17.lesson: The unconscious speaks in the language of what you love
kabbalah.path.18.lesson: Joy is passion that has found a body
kabbalah.path.19.lesson: The mindâs final gift: calling the soul to rise
kabbalah.path.2.journey: The long descent from crown to heart
kabbalah.path.2.lesson: Wisdom crosses the abyss only through silence
kabbalah.path.20.lesson: The scholarâs path ends where the hands begin
kabbalah.path.21.lesson: The last step: making the invisible visible
kabbalah.path.3.journey: Wisdom meeting understanding â father and mother unite
kabbalah.path.3.lesson: Creation begins where two principles embrace
kabbalah.path.4.lesson: Vision must find a heart or it remains blinding light
kabbalah.path.5.lesson: True teaching is an act of unconditional giving
kabbalah.path.6.lesson: The first choice: love or fear â always love
kabbalah.path.7.lesson: Structure without compassion becomes a prison; with it, a chariot
kabbalah.path.8.lesson: True strength is mercy that knows when to say no
kabbalah.path.9.lesson: The lamp of mercy illuminates the path inward
kabbalah.sefirah.0.brief: The unknowable origin â divine will before manifestation
kabbalah.sefirah.0.meditation: Crown â the point before thought, pure will
kabbalah.sefirah.0.name: Keter
kabbalah.sefirah.0.question: What exists before I think?
kabbalah.sefirah.1.brief: Primal wisdom â the lightning flash that begins all things
kabbalah.sefirah.1.meditation: Wisdom â the flash of insight, the father principle
kabbalah.sefirah.1.name: Chokmah
kabbalah.sefirah.1.question: What is the first spark of creation?
kabbalah.sefirah.2.brief: The great mother â understanding that shapes raw wisdom into form
kabbalah.sefirah.2.meditation: Understanding â the womb that gives form to wisdom
kabbalah.sefirah.2.name: Binah
kabbalah.sefirah.2.question: How does the infinite become finite?
kabbalah.sefirah.3.brief: Unconditional love â the outpouring of grace
kabbalah.sefirah.3.meditation: Mercy â boundless giving, expansion without limit
kabbalah.sefirah.3.name: Chesed
kabbalah.sefirah.3.question: Where must I give without condition?
kabbalah.sefirah.4.brief: Sacred restriction â strength that shapes through limitation
kabbalah.sefirah.4.meditation: Strength â necessary contraction, judgment, discipline
kabbalah.sefirah.4.name: Gevurah
kabbalah.sefirah.4.question: Where must I set boundaries?
kabbalah.sefirah.5.brief: The heart center â beauty born from the marriage of giving and restraint
kabbalah.sefirah.5.meditation: Beauty â the heart that balances mercy and judgment
kabbalah.sefirah.5.name: Tiferet
kabbalah.sefirah.5.question: How do I harmonize opposites?
kabbalah.sefirah.6.brief: The fire of eternity â passion that endures beyond reason
kabbalah.sefirah.6.meditation: Victory â endurance, artistic passion, desire
kabbalah.sefirah.6.name: Netzach
kabbalah.sefirah.6.question: What drives me to persist?
kabbalah.sefirah.7.brief: The glory of surrender â mind bowing to what is greater
kabbalah.sefirah.7.name: Hod
kabbalah.sefirah.7.question: How do I serve the truth?
kabbalah.sefirah.8.brief: The foundation â where all upper forces gather before manifesting
kabbalah.sefirah.8.meditation: Foundation â the gateway between above and below
kabbalah.sefirah.8.name: Yesod
kabbalah.sefirah.8.question: What connects my inner world to outer reality?
kabbalah.sefirah.9.brief: The kingdom of the present moment â divinity in matter
kabbalah.sefirah.9.meditation: Kingdom â the physical world, embodiment, presence
kabbalah.sefirah.9.name: Malkuth
kabbalah.sefirah.9.question: Am I fully here?
korean.element.0.brief: Wood ��� the green force that pushes upward through all obstacles
korean.element.0.meaning: Growth, expansion, benevolence, the force that rises
korean.element.1.brief: Fire ��� the radiance that illuminates and transforms
korean.element.1.meaning: Transformation, passion, propriety, the force that spreads
korean.element.2.brief: Earth ��� the center that nourishes and grounds all things
korean.element.2.meaning: Stability, centering, trust, the force that holds
korean.element.3.brief: Metal ��� the sharp edge that refines and lets go
korean.element.3.meaning: Precision, righteousness, clarity, the force that contracts
korean.element.4.brief: Water ��� the deep stillness that finds its way through everything
korean.element.4.meaning: Wisdom, depth, adaptability, the force that descends
korean.festival.0.brief: Lunar New Year ��� the most important holiday, when the whole nation returns home
korean.festival.0.name: Seollal
korean.festival.0.practice: Charye (ancestral rites), sebae (bowing to elders for blessings), tteokguk (rice cake soup for gaining a year)
korean.festival.0.theme: New beginnings ��� honoring ancestors, renewing family bonds
korean.festival.1.brief: Great Full Moon ��� the first full moon of the year burns away bad luck
korean.festival.1.name: Daeboreum
korean.festival.1.practice: Cracking nuts (bureom), eating five-grain rice (ogokbap), moon-viewing, fire festivals (daljip burning)
korean.festival.1.theme: Fullness ��� the first full moon, wishing for a bountiful year
korean.festival.2.brief: Double Third ��� the swallows return from the south and spring declares itself
korean.festival.2.name: Samjinnal
korean.festival.2.practice: Flower pancakes (hwajeon), spring outings, welcoming the swallows
korean.festival.2.theme: Spring arrival ��� swallows return, nature awakens
korean.festival.3.brief: Double Fifth ��� when heaven's yang energy is strongest and the earth celebrates with wrestling
korean.festival.3.name: Dano
korean.festival.3.practice: Ssireum (wrestling), swing riding, washing hair in iris water, surichwitteok (herb rice cakes)
korean.festival.3.theme: Peak yang energy ��� the most yang day of the year (5th day, 5th month)
korean.festival.4.brief: Star Festival ��� the celestial lovers' once-a-year meeting across the bridge of magpies
korean.festival.4.name: Chilseok
korean.festival.4.practice: Wishing upon stars, wheat flour noodles (milguksu), bathing in star-blessed rain
korean.festival.4.theme: Love and reunion ��� the Weaver and Cowherd cross the Milky Way
korean.festival.5.brief: Harvest Moon ��� Korea's Thanksgiving, when the full autumn moon shines on shared abundance
korean.festival.5.name: Chuseok
korean.festival.5.practice: Songpyeon (half-moon rice cakes), charye (ancestral rites), visiting graves (seongmyo), folk games
korean.festival.5.theme: Gratitude ��� harvest thanksgiving, abundance shared with ancestors
korean.festival.6.brief: Double Ninth ��� ascending to high ground to see far, drinking the flower of longevity
korean.festival.6.name: Jungyangjeol
korean.festival.6.practice: Climbing mountains, chrysanthemum wine (gukhwaju), chrysanthemum pancakes, poetry
lao.festival.1.brief: Pi Mai ��� three days when Laos washes away the old year: Sangkhan Luang (farewell), Sangkhan Nao (the day between), Sangkhan Khuen (new dawn)
lao.festival.1.name: Pi Mai Lao
lao.festival.1.practice: Pouring water over Buddha images and elders, baci string-tying ceremony, sand stupas at temples, Nang Sangkhan maiden procession in Luang Prabang
lao.festival.1.theme: Water, renewal, baci ��� the Lao New Year cleansing
lao.festival.2.brief: National Day ��� honoring the establishment of the Lao PDR and the long path to sovereignty
lao.festival.2.name: National Day
lao.festival.2.practice: National ceremonies, public holiday, reflection on independence (December 2, 1975)
lao.festival.2.theme: Sovereignty, memory ��� founding of the Lao People's Democratic Republic
lao.festival.3.brief: Liberation Day ��� the moment Laos declared its independence from colonial administration
lao.festival.3.name: Liberation Day
lao.festival.3.practice: Ceremonies and remembrance of Lao Issara independence declaration (October 12, 1945)
lao.festival.3.theme: Freedom, history ��� liberation from French colonial rule
lao.month.1.brief: January ��� named from Capricorn, the cool season wraps the river valleys
lao.month.1.season: Cool season peak ��� the sea-goat month, Mekong mists, quiet temples
lao.month.10.brief: October ��� named from Libra, Lai Heua Fai fire boats float down the Mekong
lao.month.10.season: Rainy season ending ��� the scales month, Ok Phansa (Lent ends), boat racing
lao.month.11.brief: November ��� named from Scorpio, the nation gathers at That Luang in Vientiane
lao.month.11.season: Cool season begins ��� the scorpion month, Boun That Luang at the great stupa
lao.month.12.brief: December ��� named from Sagittarius, Laos celebrates its founding and the cool returns
lao.month.12.season: Cool season ��� the archer month, National Day (December 2), year closing
lao.month.2.brief: February ��� named from Aquarius, monks chant the tale of the Buddha's last birth
lao.month.2.season: Cool season fading ��� the water-bearer month, Boun Pha Vet (Vessantara Jataka)
lao.month.3.brief: March ��� named from Pisces, the land waits between harvests
lao.month.3.season: Hot season begins ��� the fish month, dry heat over rice paddies
lao.month.4.brief: April ��� named from Aries, Nang Sangkhan descends as Laos washes away the old year
lao.month.4.season: Hot season peak ��� the ram month, Pi Mai Lao water festival
lao.month.5.brief: May ��� named from Taurus, villages launch rockets to summon the monsoon
lao.month.5.season: Hot season end ��� the bull month, Boun Bang Fai rockets call the rain
lao.month.6.brief: June ��� named from Gemini, the Buddha's birth, awakening, and parinibbana honored
lao.month.6.season: Rainy season begins ��� the twins month, Visakha Boucha (triple anniversary)
lao.month.7.brief: July ��� named from Cancer, monks enter the three-month rains retreat
lao.month.7.season: Rainy season ��� the crab month, Khao Phansa (Buddhist Lent begins)
lao.month.8.brief: August ��� named from Leo, the monsoon feeds the river and the paddies
lao.month.8.season: Rainy season peak ��� the lion month, rice transplanting, Mekong rises
lao.month.9.brief: September ��� named from Virgo, offerings placed at temples for the departed
lao.month.9.season: Late rainy season ��� the maiden month, Khao Phadap Din (honoring ancestors)
myanmar.month.0.brief: First month ��� the great water festival washes away the old year
myanmar.month.0.festival: Thingyan water festival, Myanmar New Year celebrations
myanmar.month.0.name: Tagu
myanmar.month.1.brief: Month of the Bodhi tree ��� water poured on sacred trees on the full moon
myanmar.month.1.festival: Watering the Bodhi tree (Kason full moon, Buddha's enlightenment)
myanmar.month.1.name: Kason
myanmar.month.10.brief: Month of sticky rice ��� communities gather to cook htamane together in great pots
myanmar.month.10.name: Tabodwe
myanmar.month.11.brief: Month of pagodas ��� sand stupas built on riverbanks, the great Shwedagon festival
myanmar.month.11.festival: Sand pagoda building, Shwedagon pagoda festival
myanmar.month.11.name: Tabaung
myanmar.month.2.brief: Month of study ��� the rains begin and monks deepen their learning
myanmar.month.2.name: Nayon
myanmar.month.3.brief: Month of Lent ��� Vassa begins, monks retreat for three months of rain
myanmar.month.3.festival: Waso full moon: beginning of Vassa (Lent), robe offering to monks
myanmar.month.3.name: Waso
myanmar.month.4.brief: Month of lots ��� communities draw lots to offer robes during the rains
myanmar.month.4.festival: Drawing lots for robes, spirit propitiation, nat festivals
myanmar.month.4.name: Wagaung
myanmar.month.5.brief: Month of boat races ��� the rivers are full and boats race on the floodwaters
myanmar.month.5.festival: Boat racing festivals on rivers and lakes
myanmar.month.5.name: Tawthalin
myanmar.month.6.brief: Month of lights ��� the Buddha descends from heaven, the world lights up to welcome Him
myanmar.month.6.festival: Festival of Lights (Thadingyut full moon), end of Vassa, lighting festivals
myanmar.month.6.name: Thadingyut
myanmar.month.7.brief: Month of weaving ��� all-night robe-weaving contests in the light of the full moon
myanmar.month.7.festival: Tazaungdaing festival of lights, robe-weaving competitions
myanmar.month.7.name: Tazaungmon
myanmar.month.8.brief: Month of nat spirits ��� the spirits are honored with music and offerings
myanmar.month.8.festival: Nat spirit festivals, traditional music and dance
myanmar.month.8.name: Nadaw
myanmar.month.9.brief: Month of harvest ��� the rice is gathered and the villages celebrate with games
myanmar.month.9.festival: Harvest festival, village feasts, equestrian events
myanmar.month.9.name: Pyatho

persian.festival.1.brief: The oldest continuously celebrated holiday on Earth ��� 3000+ years of welcoming spring
persian.festival.1.name: Nowruz
persian.festival.1.practice: Haft Sin table with seven symbolic items, visiting family, wearing new clothes, goldfish and sprouted wheat
persian.festival.1.theme: New Day ��� rebirth of the world at the vernal equinox
persian.festival.2.brief: Nature Day ��� leaving bad luck behind by spending the day under the open sky
persian.festival.2.name: Sizdah Bedar
persian.festival.2.practice: Picnicking outdoors, tying grass knots for wishes, releasing the sprouted sabzeh into running water
persian.festival.2.theme: Thirteenth Outside ��� spending the 13th day of the new year in nature
persian.festival.3.brief: The night of Yalda ��� red fruits against the darkness, poetry against silence
persian.festival.3.name: Yalda
persian.festival.3.practice: Staying awake all night with family, eating pomegranates and watermelon, reading Hafez poetry (fal-e Hafez)
persian.festival.3.theme: Birth of light ��� the longest night of the year conquered by togetherness
persian.festival.4.brief: The fire festival ��� leaping over flame to burn away the old year's sorrow
persian.festival.4.name: Chaharshanbe Suri
persian.festival.4.practice: Jumping over bonfires chanting 'give me your red, take my yellow' (health for sickness), fireworks, spoon-banging
persian.festival.4.theme: Red Wednesday ��� fire festival, purification before the new year
persian.month.1.brief: Month of the guardian spirits ��� the ancestors bless the new year's beginning
persian.month.1.meaning: Guardian spirits of the righteous ��� the protective ancestors
persian.month.1.name: Farvardin
persian.month.1.season: Early spring ��� Nowruz season, new year, rebirth of nature
persian.month.10.brief: Month of the Creator ��� Ahura Mazda's own month, when light begins its return
persian.month.10.meaning: The Creator himself ��� three days of this month bear his name
persian.month.10.name: Dey
persian.month.10.season: Early winter ��� solstice past, the creator's presence in stillness
persian.month.11.brief: Month of good mind ��� the righteous thought that discerns truth from lie
persian.month.11.meaning: The Amesha Spenta of good thought, right thinking, and animals
persian.month.11.name: Bahman
persian.month.11.season: Midwinter ��� contemplation, the discipline of good thought
persian.month.12.brief: Month of holy devotion ��� the sacred earth preparing to bloom once more
persian.month.12.meaning: The Amesha Spenta of the earth, devotion, and loving faith
persian.month.12.name: Esfand
persian.month.12.season: Late winter ��� Chaharshanbe Suri, fire leaping, spring anticipated
persian.month.2.brief: Month of best truth ��� the cosmic order made visible in blossoming nature
persian.month.2.meaning: The Amesha Spenta of truth, righteousness, and holy fire
persian.month.2.name: Ordibehesht
persian.month.2.season: Mid-spring ��� the earth warms, truth becomes visible in green
persian.month.3.brief: Month of wholeness ��� the perfection of the waters that sustain all life
persian.month.3.meaning: The Amesha Spenta of water, health, and perfection
persian.month.3.name: Khordad
persian.month.3.season: Late spring ��� waters flowing, fullness of the season
persian.month.4.brief: Month of Sirius ��� the star whose heliacal rising brings the longed-for rain
persian.month.4.meaning: The yazata of rain and the star Sirius, bringer of the monsoon
persian.month.4.name: Tir
persian.month.4.season: Early summer ��� heat begins, rain prayers
persian.month.5.brief: Month of immortality ��� the deathless force that lives in every green thing
persian.month.5.meaning: The Amesha Spenta of plants, immortality, and deathlessness
persian.month.5.name: Mordad
persian.month.5.season: Midsummer ��� heat at peak, plants in full power
persian.month.6.brief: Month of desirable dominion ��� the just sovereignty that rules the sky
persian.month.6.meaning: The Amesha Spenta of metals, sky, and righteous power
persian.month.6.name: Shahrivar
persian.month.6.season: Late summer ��� harvest approaching, the kingdom of light
persian.month.7.brief: Month of Mithra ��� the lord of the covenant who watches over every promise
persian.month.7.meaning: The yazata of oath, friendship, and the sun's justice
persian.month.7.name: Mehr
persian.month.7.season: Early autumn ��� equinox, balance of light and dark
persian.month.8.brief: Month of the waters ��� the cleansing rains that renew the parched earth
persian.month.8.meaning: The yazata of the cosmic waters and purification
persian.month.8.name: Aban
persian.month.9.brief: Month of fire ��� the sacred flame that holds back the longest night
persian.month.9.meaning: The yazata of fire, the sacred flame of Ahura Mazda
persian.month.9.name: Azar
persian.month.9.season: Late autumn ��� Yalda approaches, fire against the growing dark
tamil.festival.1.brief: Tamil New Year ��� 1 Chittirai, when the sidereal Sun crosses into Mesha
tamil.festival.1.name: Puthandu
tamil.festival.1.practice: Viewing the kanni (auspicious tray), mango pachadi (sweet-sour-bitter dish symbolizing life's tastes), visiting temples
tamil.festival.1.theme: New Year ��� the sidereal Sun enters Aries, auspicious beginnings
tamil.festival.2.brief: The harvest festival ��� four days in Thai month when abundance is celebrated with overflowing pots
tamil.festival.2.name: Pongal
tamil.festival.2.practice: Boiling rice in new pots until it overflows (pongal = overflowing), decorating cows, kolam art, bonfires
tamil.festival.2.theme: Harvest thanksgiving ��� gratitude to the Sun, cows, and the earth
tamil.festival.3.brief: The celestial wedding ��� Madurai's great festival celebrating the goddess who married Shiva
tamil.festival.3.name: Chithirai Festival
tamil.festival.3.practice: Ten-day festival in Madurai, chariot procession, re-enactment of the divine marriage
tamil.festival.3.theme: Divine marriage ��� Meenakshi and Sundareswarar's cosmic wedding
tamil.festival.4.brief: The festival of lights ��� every lamp declares that darkness cannot endure
tamil.festival.4.name: Deepavali
tamil.festival.4.practice: Oil lamps, firecrackers, new clothes, sweets, oil bath at dawn, family gatherings
tamil.festival.4.theme: Victory of light ��� triumph over darkness, the return of righteousness
tamil.month.1.brief: First month ��� the Sun enters sidereal Aries and the Tamil year begins
tamil.month.10.brief: Month of Pongal ��� the harvest thanksgiving, the Sun's northward journey begins
tamil.month.11.brief: Month of the water-bearer ��� Maha Shivaratri, the great night of Shiva
tamil.month.12.brief: Last month ��� Panguni Uthiram (divine weddings), the fish rasi closes the year
tamil.month.2.brief: Month of temple celebrations ��� the bull rasi, steadfast under the blazing sun
tamil.month.3.brief: The twins month ��� the monsoon arrives and the parched earth drinks
tamil.month.4.brief: Month of Aadi ��� the crab rasi, monsoon fullness, Aadi Perukku (river festival)
tamil.month.5.brief: Month of the lion ��� Avani Avittam (sacred thread renewal) and monsoon bounty
tamil.month.6.brief: Month of Navaratri ��� nine nights of the goddess, the maiden rasi
tamil.month.7.brief: Month of the scales ��� Deepavali season, the balance between light and dark
tamil.month.8.brief: Month of Karthigai ��� the lamp festival, the Pleiades at zenith
tamil.month.8.season: Festival of lights, cool season begins
tamil.month.9.brief: Holiest month ��� Margazhi kolams (rice flour art) at dawn, the archer rasi
thai.festival.1.brief: The water festival ��� three days when Thailand washes away the old year with joy and reverence
thai.festival.1.name: Songkran
thai.festival.1.practice: Pouring water over Buddha images and elders' hands, water fights in the streets, building sand stupas at temples
thai.festival.1.theme: Water, renewal, respect ��� the Thai New Year cleansing
thai.festival.2.brief: Chakri Memorial Day ��� honoring King Rama I who founded Bangkok and the dynasty
thai.festival.2.name: Chakri Day
thai.festival.2.practice: Ceremony at the Grand Palace, paying respects to past kings, national holiday
thai.festival.2.theme: Dynasty, gratitude ��� honoring the founding of the Chakri royal house
thai.festival.3.brief: Constitution Day ��� remembering the transition from absolute to constitutional monarchy
thai.festival.3.name: Constitution Day
thai.festival.3.practice: National ceremonies, public holiday, reflection on democratic principles
thai.festival.3.theme: Democracy, nationhood ��� commemorating Thailand's first constitution
thai.month.1.brief: January ��� named from Capricorn, the cool season reaches its depth
thai.month.1.season: Cool season peak ��� the sea-goat month, harvest of cool-season crops
thai.month.10.brief: October ��� named from Libra, balance returns as the rains recede
thai.month.10.season: Rainy season ending ��� the scales month, Lent ends (Ok Phansa)
thai.month.11.brief: November ��� named from Scorpio, floating lanterns honor the river goddess
thai.month.11.season: Cool season begins ��� the scorpion month, Loy Krathong festival
thai.month.12.brief: December ��� named from Sagittarius, the cool season's gift
thai.month.12.season: Cool season ��� the archer month, Father's Day (King's birthday)
thai.month.2.brief: February ��� named from Aquarius, the last cool breath before heat
thai.month.2.season: Cool season fading ��� the water-bearer month, Chinese New Year festivities
thai.month.3.brief: March ��� named from Pisces, the transition from cool to scorching
thai.month.3.season: Hot season begins ��� the fish month, dry heat building
thai.month.4.brief: April ��� named from Aries, the hottest month cooled by Songkran's water
thai.month.4.season: Hot season peak ��� the ram month, Songkran water festival
thai.month.5.brief: May ��� named from Taurus, the earth waits for rain
thai.month.5.season: Hot season end ��� the bull month, monsoon anticipation
thai.month.6.brief: June ��� named from Gemini, the monsoon announces itself
thai.month.6.season: Rainy season begins ��� the twins month, first monsoon rains
thai.month.7.brief: July ��� named from Cancer, monks enter the rains retreat
thai.month.7.season: Rainy season ��� the crab month, Buddhist Lent (Khao Phansa) begins
thai.month.8.brief: August ��� named from Leo, the rains intensify
thai.month.8.season: Rainy season peak ��� the lion month, Mother's Day (Queen's birthday)
thai.month.9.brief: September ��� named from Virgo, the rice paddies glow emerald
thai.month.9.season: Late rainy season ��� the maiden month, rice fields green
